rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CLIFFORD A. HELLWIG REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR APPOINTMENT OF SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Court to determine whether proper venue has been established. 2) Court to determine whether bond should be required. A verified supplemental declaration alleging the fair market value of the trust assets should be filed prior to the hearing date. 3) Court to determine whether an alternate successor trustee can be named at this time, or if such action should be considered an amendment to the trust, which would require another proceeding in this matter. 4) Court to determine whether Petitioner should be named successor trustee. 5) Petitioner requests that various costs be allowed to be paid by the trust (including trustee and attorney fees); however, Petitioner has not alleged why such an order is necessary. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSANNE CAPALBO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The copy of the Partial #1 I&A attached to the petition is incomplete. (Item 4 of the I&A was answered, although a Probate Referee WAS appointed.) No Final I&A was filed. Paragraph 24 of the report appears to be incomplete. Notwithstanding the waivers of accounting, the petition still refers to an account in the caption and an "accounting summary" in the body, but there is no summary of account and no proper detailed accounting schedules attached. The petition does not disclose what actions were taken under the administrator's IAEA authority. It appears that she sold and/or purchased securities; the assets on hand differ from the assets on the date of death. [CRC 7.550(b)(2).] Did she give Notice of Proposed Action to sell the real property and donate the vehicle to a friend? A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] Distribution "in-kind" or in total market values, etc., is not sufficient. Probate Code Section 1064(a)(5) requires an allegation disclosing whether all of the cash has been invested and maintained in interest bearing accounts or in investments authorized by law or the governing instrument, etc., not just that cash was retained in an estate bank account. There was a creditor's claim filed on 4/13/21. That calculation of the statutory fee is not based on an "adjusted estate value;" it must begin with the total assets on the date of death, plus income receipts and gains on sales, and minus losses on sales. Detailed schedules of receipts and gains and losses on sale must be filed to support the statutory fee. [CRC 7.705(b) and 7.550(b)(6).] The administrator is seeking compensation for extraordinary services, but this was not disclosed in the petition caption. [CRC 7.102.] Furthermore, this Court does not ordinarily permit more than $45/hour for non-professional fiduciaries without a showing of special skills necessary to perform such services. The hours shown on the time sheets (Schedule 3) do not appear to total 110, and most of the time appears to be ordinary services. Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. A general personal representative SHALL give notice to the FTB. [Probate Code Section 9202(c)(1). A petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.36 I.2.] If notice has not yet been given to the FTB, the hearing must be continued at least 60 days unless the FTB waives notice or otherwise communicates that the estate owes no taxes. It appears that an amended petition must be filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 11/28/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TIMOTHY A. CRAMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/16/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. This court views the Probate Code as requiring a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report of status to be filed or the personal representative may file a petition for termination based on there being no assets. The matter will not be dismissed based on a request for dismissal. Continued again for counsel to comply with filing a petition for termination or a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report of status of administration.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SARAH BURNS-TURNER

rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DR. CYNTHIA Y. NG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/15/2022. Additional notes on the "revised" petition were e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/14/2022, even though no courtesy copy had been received. Note: Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO SAN DIEGO COUNTY**IN THE MATTER OF THE TERESITA H. RAVETTI SEPARATE PROPERTY TRUST AGREEMENT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Remove A Co-Trustee And Compel An Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/13/2022 for judicial economy, to provide required 30 days' notice and to explain why San Francisco is the proper venue. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Petitioner must give 30 days notice to all trustees, heirs, and beneficiaries on the mandatory judicial council form DE-115 and include the required language in Local Rule 14.1 D. 6) Please explain the legal basis for San Francisco being the proper venue. Based on the filings it appears proper venue is San Diego.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARGANETTA L. FINNEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/15/22 for failure to address Examiner's Notes. All required documents to be filed and provided by 10/31/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KONRAD KRAUSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY

rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. Petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report of status of administration must be filed by 10/24/2023. If not on file, appearance required at status hearing set for 11/07/2023.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES LEO NEIRINCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/22/22. No proposed order. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] No courtesy copies. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] The proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and all supporting papers must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 11/10/22 to avoid another continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.
